treatment_information.treatment_response: Did not prevent evolution towards pseudarthrosis

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: via anterolateral approach with iliac grafts
treatment_information.treatment_response: Improved bone trophicity but pseudarthrosis persisted

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: with recentering of the extensor apparatus by transfer of the tibial tuberosity
treatment_information.treatment_response: Consolidation obtained in four months without complications
Allowed for functional and aesthetic prosthetic fitting

treatment_timeline.event: Diagnosis of congenital pseudarthrosis of the tibia (PCT)
First surgical intervention: high intertibiofibular graft (GITP)
Second surgical intervention: tibialization of the fibula
Third surgical intervention: low intertibiofibular graft (GITP) for distal tibia fracture

patient_outcome.final_status: Satisfactory functional result with good knee mobility
patient_outcome.complications: Fatigue fracture of the distal tibia leading to pseudarthrosis, stiffness of the ankle at a right angle
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Good quality of life with social and professional integration
patient_outcome.recovery_timeline: Consolidation obtained in four months after tibialization of the fibula
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Satisfactory, engaged in sports (horse riding, skiing)
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Satisfied with the reconstruction result despite tolerable cosmetic appearance
